calendar.matters:
Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Apr-17-2018 Off Calendar

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Mar-20-2018 Continued To Apr-17-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Collins for Petitioners
David Helbraun for Dayna Yearwood (Respondent)

Mr. Collins stated that they have arrived at a settlement, and that what remains is final wording to be approved. Mr. Helbraun requested the matter be set for trial estimated at 5 days. Mr. Helbraun stated that expert depositions still need to be completed. The Petition to Determine Validity of Purported Trust and to Impose Constructive Trust is continued to April 17, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery and settlement.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Mar-06-2018 Continued To Mar-20-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Feb-06-2018 Continued To Mar-06-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Collins for petitioners Desiree Valverde and Heather Hawkins. No appearance by David Helbraun.

Mr. Collins reported that the parties were presently engaged in settlement discussions and were making progress towards resolving the matter. The Court noted his appreciation that settlement efforts were underqay, but was surprised that Mr. Helbraun was not present at today's hearing. The matter was continued to March 6, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. He directed the filing of a joint status statement by the parties by February 27, 2018, unless the dispute was settled and the petition taken off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Jan-22-2018 Continued To Feb-06-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Respondents Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-14-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Charles M. Collins for the Petitioners. David M. Helbraun for the Respondents / Moving Parties.

Matter called, argument held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: The motion for summary judgment is denied. The declaration of Dr. Aazaz Haq is sufficient to raise a triable issue of material fact as to Ms. Yearwood's testamentary capacity at the times she signed her 1999 and 1996 wills. The order was signed in open court.
Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque. Matter Not Reported.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Nov-22-2017 Continued To Jan-22-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Respondent Dayna Yearwood For Attorneys Fees Under Ccp Section 405.38
Off Calendar Oct-18-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

There being no appearances, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows: The motion for an award of fees is granted. Nothing in the law requires that such a motion be brought concurrently with the motion to expunge. Given the absence of persuasive evidence showing a lack of testimonial capacity in 1999, the Court concludes that Petitioners did not have substantial justification for filing and defending the lis pendens. The Court has carefully reviewed the fee request and concludes that $15,658 is the reasonable amount of fees and costs that Petitioners Desiree Valverde and Heather Hawkins must pay to Respondent Dayna Yearwood.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Matter Not Reported.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Sep-27-2017 Continued To Nov-22-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Charles Collins for Petitioners.
David Helbraun for Dana Yearwood (Trustee/Respondent).

Mr. Collins stated that they have been in negotiation for settlement, that they are far apart at this point, that he believes next week will be a turning point once the motion for attorney's fees is heard and that after next week, they will know what the disposition is going to be. Mr. Helbraun stated that the Respondent is ready and willing to go to mediation. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than November 15, 2017. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by November 16, 2017. The Petition to Determine Validity of Purported Trust, etc. is continued to and the Parties are to return on November 22, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Jul-18-2017 Continued To Sep-27-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Charles Collins for Petitioners.
David Helbraun for and with Dayna Yearwood (Trustee).

Mr. Collins stated that he has made some inquiries to Mr. Helbraun, but he has not gotten back to him and suggested setting the matter for trial. Mr. Helbraun stated that he would like to take a deposition and that he would to have the trial set to allow him time to file a motion for summary judgment. The Court informed Counsel that it will not set the matter for trial if the parties are still litigating and asked if they were interested in mediation. The Petition to Determine Validity of Purported Trust, etc. is continued to September 27, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that by that time, the parties will have completed any discovery they plan to do and will have met and conferred about resolving the matter through settlement or mediation. If the matter is not resolved by September 20, 2017, the parties are to file a joint status conference statement preferably or file separate statements indicating where things stand, what is left to do and what the trial will look like.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens
Off Calendar Jul-12-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Collins for and with Desiree Valverde and Heather Hawkins (Petitioners).
David Helbraun for and with Dana Yearwood (Respondent).

Mr. Collins presented his oral argument in position of his opposition to the Court's tenative ruling. Mr. Helbraun stated that this is a motion to expunge lis pendens and that the burden falls on the peitioners. Having heard oral argument and reviewed court filed documents, the Court adopts the tenative ruling as follows: The motion to expunge lis pendens is granted. In light of the admmissible evidence presented by Respondents and the applicable presumptions, the evidence presented by Petitioners does not persuade the Court by a preponderance of the evidence that Petitioners' real property claim is probably valid. Petitioners have not carried their burden of showing either that Ms. Yearwood lacked testamentary (or other legal) capacity in 1999 or that her 1999 testamentary documents were the product of undue influence. Mr. Helbraun provides a form of order that tracks the tentative. In open court Mr. Collins approves the order as to form and the Court signs the order.

Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: N. Wong, Bailiff: Deputy Nuque. Court Reporter: Marilyn Miller, CSR: 3891, Telephone: 415-981-3498.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar May-16-2017 Continued To Jul-18-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Apr-25-2017 Continued To May-16-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Jan-17-2017 Continued To Apr-25-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Charles Collins for Petitioners Desiree Valverde and Heather Hawkins.
David Helbraun for and with Dana Yearwood (Respondent).

Counsel reported that documents were exchanged, that some depositions have been taken, that depositions still need to be taken and are scheduled, that there will be witnesses and that they need time/three to four months to complete discovery. Mr. Helbraun stated that there is a pour over will from 1999, that the house is the only issue, that the house involves difficult circumstances, and that he will be filing a motion to expunge lis pendens and a motion for summary judgment. Mr. Collins stated that he will amend his petition after taking Ms. Yearwood's deposition. The Petition to Determine Validity of Purported Trust and to Impose Constructive Trust is continued to April 25, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the parties to get their discovery done in hopes that if a trial is still necessary, they will be in a position to set one.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Duong. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Oct-12-2016 Continued To Jan-17-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Jul-13-2016 Continued To Oct-12-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Collins for (petitioners) Desiree Valverde & Heather Hawkins. David Helbraun for and with (trustee) Dayna Yearwood.

The Court confirmed that the petitioners are raising issue as to capacity, that the trust was executed in 1999 and asked how there could be incapacity 10 years later. Mr. Collins reported that it is based on observance by family members and friends, that Ms. Yearwood had difficulty prior to 1999, that they would like to do discovery to obtain medical records for confirmation and if necessary go to trial. The Court asked if counsel was aware of any medical examinations, anything other than the observations by family & friends. Mr. Collins stated that the petitioners were kept from their mother by Dayna Yearwood, that they had no way of finding out about medical information, that Ms. Yearwood was placed in a motel in San Mateo prior to her death and was cremated with no autopsy. The Court asked where Desiree and Heather were living. Mr. Collins reported that Desiree lived in the bottom half of the house, that Ms. Yearwood lived upstairs, that the locks were changed and Desiree was prevented from having contact with her mother, that Dayna Yearwood took control of Ms. Yearwoods life, that the petitioners should be given the opportunity to prove incapacity prior to the signing of the trust, that Ms. Yearwoods signature on the trust was not notarized, that Ms. Yearwood always said that her daughters would inherit equally and never stated anything to the contrary. Mr. Helbraun stated that they disagree, that Ms. Yearwood made the trust and Power of Attorney is 1999 and was fully competent, that the drafting attorney, Thomas Feeney will support this, that medical records for procedures done after 1999 will show that Ms. Yearwood gave consent and was not incapacitated, that the petitioners will not be able to bear their burden, that the trustee will object and respond to charges and that there are friends available who will say that Ms. Yearwood was fully competent. Mr. Collins said that those are hearsay statements. The Court stated that both parties are making hearsay statements. Mr. Collins stated that they would like to obtain the medical records, that they would like to depose the doctors and that Ms. Yearwood did peculiar things such as cooking peculiar meals. The Court asked if there were concerns by Desiree & Heather regarding their mother being subjected to undue influence why did they not do something during the 10 years. Mr. Collins responded that they tried to obtain a key to the house and that their mother was kept away from the other children. The Court took the petition to determine validity of purported trust and to impose constructive trust under submission.

Subsequent to the hearing the matter was un-submitted and continued for 90 days to October 12, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for discovery and trial setting. The parties are to be notified by the Probate Examiner.

Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
